headlines.title: Global Oil Prices Rise as Fresh U.S. Strikes on Iran Cast Shadow Over Trump's Promised Peace Deal
Another surge for Micron, Wall Street's latest US$1 trillion company, sends U.S. stocks to records
US-Israel Strike Iran Vessels at Hormuz, Commodities Whip
Treasury Yields Fall to Kick Off the Week
Prospects fade for imminent end to Iran war as attacks restart

notes: US equity markets hit record closing highs today following the Memorial Day holiday weekend, heavily driven by continued tech and AI rallies, pushing the S&P 500 and Nasdaq higher.
Brent crude oil rallied significantly following new US-Israel military strikes on Iranian targets near the Strait of Hormuz, throwing cold water on earlier diplomatic optimism.
Treasury yields eased marginally over the session despite resurfacing inflation fears tied to the oil shock.
